zh.lifeSummary: 當Philip de Braose於1073年出生在英國素可布拉波 卡素時，他的父親William de Braose24歲，母親Agnes de St. Clare29歲。他於1104年與Eleanor de Totnes在英國素可結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1134年登記服兵役。他去世於1134年5月在دولتْ علیّه عثمانیّه耶路撒冷享年61歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭諾斯 艾斯爾霍利。

nameMeanings.name: Braose
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Eckhard, Gerhard, Manfred, Otto.
from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Ambrosius (see Ambrose ).

variants.name: Ambrose
meanings: English and Scottish: from the personal name Ambrose (French Ambroise, Latin Ambrosius, from Greek ambrosios ‘immortal, divine’), which was popular throughout Christendom in medieval Europe. Its popularity was due in part to the fame of Saint Ambrose (c. 340–397), one of the four Latin Fathers of the Church, the teacher of Saint Augustine. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Czech, Slovenian, and Croatian Ambrož (see Ambroz ), and also their derivatives, e.g. the Slovenian patronymic Ambrožič.
Irish: from Mac Ambróis ‘son of Ambrose’ (see 1 above); a West Munster name, which has also been Anglicized as McCambridge .

nameMeanings.name: Philip
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Greek name Philippos, meaning ‘lover of horses’, from philein ‘to love’ + hippos ‘horse’. This was popular in the classical period and since. It was the name of the father of Alexander the Great . It was also the name of one of Christ's apostles, of a deacon ordained by the apostles after the death of Christ, and of several other early saints. See also Philippa .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Philip de Braose was born in 1073, in Bramber Castle, Sussex, England, his father, William de Braose, was 24 and his mother, Agnes de St. Clare, was 29. He married Eleanor de Totnes in 1104, in Sussex, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. He registered for military service in 1134. He died in May 1134, in Jerusalem, Ottoman Empire, at the age of 61, and was buried in Holy, North Ayrshire, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Philip de Braose est né en 1073, à Bramber Castle, Sussex, Angleterre, son père, William de Braose, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Agnes de St. Clare, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Eleanor de Totnes en 1104, à Sussex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1134. Il est décédé en mai 1134, à Jerusalem, Empire ottoman, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterré à Holy, North Ayrshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Philip de Braose 1073 in Bramber Castle, Sussex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de Braose 24 und seine Mutter Agnes de St. Clare 29. Er hat 1104 in Sussex, England Eleanor de Totnes geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er hat sich 1134 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb im Mai 1134 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Jerusalem, Osmanisches Reich und wurde in Holy, North Ayrshire, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Philip de Braoseが1073年にイギリススセックスブランバー カストルで生まれた時，父William de Braoseは24歳，母Agnes de St. Clareは29歳であった。1104年にイギリススセックスでEleanor de Totnesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1134年に兵役登録をした。彼は1134年5月にオスマン帝国ジャーウサレムで61歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドノース エイルシュアイアホーリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Philip de Braose(이)가 1073년 영국 수세스 브램버 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Braose(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes de St. Clare(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1104년 영국 수세스에서 Eleanor de Totnes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1134년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1134년 5월 دولتْ علیّه عثمانیّه 제루사렘에서 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 노르스 에이르실르 홀이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Philip de Braose nasceu em 1073, em Bramber Castle, Sussex, Inglaterra, seu pai, William de Braose, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Agnes de St. Clare, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Eleanor de Totnes em 1104, em Sussex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 1 filha. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1134. Ele faleceu em maio de 1134, em Jerusalem, Império Otomano, com 61 anos, e foi sepultado em Holy, North Ayrshire, Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Philip de Braose родился в 1073 г. в местности брэмбир кастл, суссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени William de Braose было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes de St. Clare, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Eleanor de Totnes в 1104 г. в местности суссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. В 1134 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер май 1134 г. в местности джирсеилем, Османская империя в возрасте 61 год и был похоронен в местности холи, норт аиршир, Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Philip de Braose nació en 1073, en Bramber Castle, Sussex, Inglaterra, su papá, William de Braose, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Agnes de St. Clare, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Eleanor de Totnes en 1104, en Sussex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1134. Murió en mayo de 1134, en Jerusalem, Imperio otomano, a la edad de 61 años, y fue sepultado en Holy, North Ayrshire, Escocia, Reino Unido.
